endpoints.description: Ctrip train route SSR page. The HTML contains __NEXT_DATA__ with trainInfoList; browser_api_call compacts it into route, trainCount, pricedSecondClassCount, fastest train by runMinutes, and cheapest train by secondClassPrice.
strategy_notes: Construct the route URL directly as https://trains.ctrip.com/trainbooking/<fromSlug>-<toSlug>?date=<YYYY-MM-DD>.
Use browser_api_call with fetch_mode=http on each route URL instead of navigating route pages one by one.
For batch train tasks, compute fastest from runMinutes and cheapest from secondClassPrice from the compact route summary, then save the collected JSON.

runtime_strategies.name: ctrip_train_batch_route_contract
runtime_strategies.trigger: Ctrip train tasks asking for route ticket data, fastest trains, cheapest second-class prices, or repeated city-pair collection.

actions.description: Call the compact train route page for each requested city pair and date.

observable_validation: Compact route result includes a nonzero trainCount and pricedSecondClassCount.
fastest.runMinutes and cheapest.secondClassPrice are present before saving the route result.
notes: Do not scroll Ctrip route pages for batch collection once compact route data is available.
If a route slug is uncertain, open one visible route page to verify the slug, then continue through compact route calls.
